# terrible_telekom_mms_icinga_director_icinga_command (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `object_name` (String) Name of the command.

### Optional

- `append` (Boolean) Do not overwrite the whole object but instead append the defined properties. Note - Appending to existing vars, imports or any other list/dict is not possible. You have to overwrite the complete list/dict. Note - Variables that are set by default will also be applied, even if not set.
- `arguments` (String) Arguments of the command. Each argument can take either a string, a json or a dict When using a dict as argument value, the following properties are supported. `skip_key`, `repeat_key`, `required`, `order`, `description`), `set_if`, `value`. The `value` property can be either a string, a json or a dict. When used as a dict, you can define its `type` as `Function` and set its `body` property as an Icinga DSL piece of config.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `command` (String) The command Icinga should run. Required when state is `present`. Absolute paths are accepted as provided, relative paths are prefixed with "PluginDir + ", similar Constant prefixes are allowed. Spaces will lead to separation of command path and standalone arguments. Please note that this means that we do not support spaces in plugin names and paths right now.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disabled objects will not be deployed.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `imports` (String) Importable templates, add as many as you want. Please note that order matters when importing properties from multiple templates - last one wins.
- `methods_execute` (String) Plugin Check commands are what you need when running checks against your infrastructure. Notification commands will be used when it comes to notify your users. Event commands allow you to trigger specific actions when problems occur. Some people use them for auto-healing mechanisms, like restarting services or rebooting systems at specific thresholds.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Apply feature state.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `vars` (String) Custom properties of the command.
- `zone` (String) Icinga cluster zone. Allows to manually override Directors decisions of where to deploy your config to. You should consider not doing so unless you gained deep understanding of how an Icinga Cluster stack works.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
